content.problemStatement: Rectify the error in the code. The program is supposed to take two integer inputs and print their division.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers a and b (1 ≤ a, b ≤ 1000, b ≠ 0).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the integer result of floor division a / b.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. 5 divided by 5 equals 1.
One test case. 10 divided by 2 equals 5.
One test case. 7 divided by 3 is 2 with remainder 1; integer division discards the remainder.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read integers a and b. Because all numbers are positive, the language's integer division operator already yields the floor of the exact quotient. Output a/b for each case on its own line. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time division per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables besides the output buffer.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int a,b; scanf("%d %d",&a,&b); printf("%d\n", a/b);} return 0;}
code.Python3:
import sys
data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T=int(data[0])
idx=1
out=[]
for _ in range(T):
    a=int(data[idx]); b=int(data[idx+1]); idx+=2
    out.append(str(a//b))
print('\n'.join(out))
